methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
WB Group is a pre-IPO stage company in the Defense & Security sector. The comparable universe consists of 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. However, the peer set presents material constraints for valuation.

The peer-median approach is not applicable. Of 21 same-sector peers identified in Defense & Security, none satisfy the stricter filters of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier simultaneously. Achieving a minimum of three datapoints within a narrowly comparable cohort proved infeasible. An all-sector median would introduce uncontrolled variability in growth profiles, margins, and risk profiles, rendering such a calculation unreliable. No triangulated valuation range can therefore be anchored with confidence using conventional comparable-company methods.

Without sufficient narrow peers meeting stage and scale criteria, traditional multiples-based valuation cannot be responsibly applied. Forward-looking DCF analysis or transaction-based precedent analysis would require material assumptions beyond the provided dataset. The absence of disclosed fundraising history and current valuation further constrains inference. A credible valuation narrative requires either: narrowing the peer screen by additional operating metrics, obtaining margin or revenue growth profiles for comparable firms, or shifting to transaction multiples from recent Defense & Security financings. Current data is insufficient to triangulate a defensible range.
